lever_summary: Extend the dense i64 elementwise fast paths beyond the pass67 Add: generalize the same-shape I64 fast path to any I64xI64 binop (the dispatcher's int_op carries per-primitive semantics for Sub/Mul/Div/Rem/Max/Min/Pow), and add an i64 scalar-broadcast fast path in both operand orders. Both fold LiteralBuffer::as_i64_slice() and emit dense i64.

alien_graveyard.primitive: dense SoA / typed columnar storage applied to elementwise binops
alien_graveyard.mapping: consume the contiguous i64 backing store (mcqr.30.13) across the full integer elementwise op set instead of per-element tagged-union dispatch via binary_literal_op

method: Same-worker comparison on ts2: dense (vector_i64) inputs vs Vec<Literal>-backed reference benches run in the same process, isolating the data-model win from cross-worker CPU variance.

scalar_mul_64k_i64.note: headline: the generic scalar-broadcast path runs the heavy binary_literal_op (promote_dtype + literal_to_i128 + i64::try_from) per element; the dense path is a tight int_op fold

command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 64k_i64 --save-baseline i64elem

change.lever: Renamed eval_same_shape_i64_add -> eval_same_shape_i64_binop and dropped the Add-only guard at the call site (fires for any I64xI64 same-shape). Added eval_i64_scalar_broadcast_binop(scalar, tensor, scalar_on_left, int_op) wired into both Scalar(*)Tensor and Tensor(*)Scalar arms after the F64 broadcast attempt. Both fall back to the unchanged generic path when not dense i64.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: slices folded in element order; scalar broadcast preserves operand order via scalar_on_left for non-commutative Sub/Div
isomorphism.integer_op: unchanged: for I64xI64, binary_literal_op returns Literal::I64(int_op(a,b)); the fast paths apply the same dispatcher int_op (Add wrapping_add, Sub wrapping_sub, Mul wrapping_mul, Div checked_div().unwrap_or(0), etc.), so wrapping/overflow behavior is identical
isomorphism.dtype_promotion: unchanged: promote_dtype(I64, I64) == I64; fast paths emit new_i64_values (I64)
isomorphism.errors: as_i64_slice() is Some only for I64 dense storage; mixed/Literal-backed/malformed-I64 and non-I64 scalar inputs return None and run the unchanged generic loop (which still produces the identical Literal::I64 result for I64xI64)

validation.name: i64_elementwise_parity
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax --lib dense_i64
validation.result: passed: dense_i64_sub_mul_and_scalar_broadcast_bit_identical_to_literal_path (Sub/Mul same-shape + scalar broadcast both orders, incl i64::MIN/MAX wrapping) + the pass67 add and pass68 reduce parity tests

validation.name: full_fj_lax_lib_suite
validation.result: passed: 1049 tests (generalized same-shape i64 path, no regressions)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
